title: Rate hike fears grow as inflation surges
full_text: Other Editions 01 May 2026 News Rate hike fears grow as inflation surges Daily Telegraph Jessica Wang 30 April 2026 217 words English DAITEL 5 © News Pty Limited. No redistribution is permitted. Treasurer Jim Chalmers has warned the worst is yet to come after inflation reached a three-year high of 4.6 per cent yesterday, leaving the RBA little choice but to “hit the breaks” and hike rates. Economists warned the real pain from the fuel crisis was yet to hit, which would bring price hikes across the economy. Yesterday’s inflation update for the March quarter, the first drop to capture the start of the Iran war, came in lower than market predictions of 4.8 per cent, with the trimmed mean – which removes volatile price rises like petrol – stable at 3.3 per cent. Dr Chalmers said Aussies were “paying a hefty price for the war in the Middle East” and vowed “spending restraint” in the upcoming budget. “The expectation is that these oil shock pressures will continue to play out in inflation,” he said. AMP chief economist Shane Oliver believed the RBA would have little option but to hike rates next week. Sydney architect Joe McLoughlin said he had cut back on the “little things” as price pressures bite. “You even have to start looking at, do I buy a coffee in the morning?” he said. summary: Australia's inflation has surged to a three-year high of 4.6%, prompting Treasurer Jim Chalmers to indicate that the Reserve Bank of Australia may need to raise interest rates. Economists warn that the ongoing fuel crisis will further exacerbate inflationary pressures. Chalmers highlighted the impact of the Middle East conflict on domestic prices and announced plans for spending restraint in the upcoming budget.

key_points: Inflation reaches 4.6%, the highest in three years
RBA likely to raise interest rates due to inflation pressures
Treasurer warns of ongoing economic pain from the fuel crisis
relevance: Understanding inflation trends and interest rate movements is crucial for actuaries and financial services professionals in managing risk and advising clients.
